name: GD Clone - Miva orders to NetSuite sales orders
description: Testing - Adding attributes and products to NetSuite when they don't exist

aiDescription.summary: This flow exports orders from Miva's eCommerce platform and imports them into NetSuite as sales orders. It also updates or creates customers in NetSuite based on their email. The flow runs every hour in the "America/Los_Angeles" timezone. It consists of three main components: "pageGenerators" for extracting data from Miva, "pageProcessors" for importing data into NetSuite, and "autoResolveMatchingTraceKeys" for resolving errors with the same trace key. The flow uses HTTP exports, field mappings, and distributed imports to perform the data transfer. It also includes pre-mapping logic for calculating discounts and shipping costs.
aiDescription.detailed:
This flow is called "Miva orders to NetSuite sales orders". It exports orders created within Miva's eCommerce platform and posts them to NetSuite as sales orders. It also updates or creates customers in NetSuite by looking them up via their email.

The flow has a schedule set to run every hour. It is configured to run in the "America/Los_Angeles" timezone.

The flow consists of three main components: "pageGenerators", "pageProcessors", and "autoResolveMatchingTraceKeys".

The "pageGenerators" component is responsible for extracting data from the Miva eCommerce platform. It uses an HTTP export to retrieve orders from Miva. The export is configured to use a delta method, which means it will only export orders that have been created or modified since the last successful export. The export is also configured to use a POST request to the "/json.mvc" endpoint of the Miva API. The request body includes parameters such as the store code, function, and filter criteria. The response from the export is expected to be in JSON format, and the exported data is located at "data.data" in the response.

The "pageProcessors" component is responsible for importing data into NetSuite. It consists of three imports: "Import NetSuite customers", "Import NetSuite customers (Guest)", and "Import NetSuite sales orders".

The "Import NetSuite customers" import is configured to add or update customers in NetSuite using the email as the lookup identifier. It includes field mappings for various customer attributes such as email, first name, last name, phone, and address. It also includes mappings for billing and shipping addresses. The import is distributed, which means it can process data in parallel for faster imports. It uses a NetSuiteDistributedImport adaptor to perform the import.

The "Import NetSuite customers (Guest)" import is similar to the previous import, but it uses the customer ID as the lookup identifier instead of the email. It also includes mappings for company name and default values for first name and last name if they are empty.

The "Import NetSuite sales orders" import is responsible for adding or ignoring sales orders in NetSuite. It uses a custom field as the lookup identifier. The import includes field mappings for various sales order attributes such as customer, shipping address, billing address, shipping method, payment method, and line items. It also includes hooks to perform pre-mapping logic, such as calculating discounts and shipping costs. The import is distributed and uses a NetSuiteDistributedImport adaptor.

The "autoResolveMatchingTraceKeys" field is set to true, which means that the flow will automatically resolve other open errors with the same trace key. This helps to avoid unnecessary errors and improve the flow's performance.

Overall, this flow exports orders from Miva and imports them into NetSuite as sales orders. It also updates or creates customers in NetSuite based on the email or customer ID. The flow is scheduled to run every hour and is configured to run in the "America/Los_Angeles" timezone.
